commit.message:
test(ipc): deep tests for transport, feature negotiation, and service communication (#142)

* test(ipc): add deep tests for transport, feature negotiation, and service communication

Add 80 new tests in ipc_transport_negotiation_tests.rs covering areas
not exercised by existing test files:

1. Feature negotiation edge cases (10 tests):
   - Re-negotiation registering multiple clients
   - Feature ordering independence
   - Duplicate feature handling
   - All 7 server features activation
   - Incompatible client behavior (not registered, still gets supported list)
   - Unicode and many unknown features
   - Timeout config and min_client_version in result

2. Transport layer behavior (14 tests):
   - Buffer size defaults and customization
   - Serde roundtrip for transport/IPC configs
   - Connection limit and timeout boundary values
   - Named pipe with long names, TCP with port 0 and IPv6
   - Server restart cycle preserving config

3. Message serialization for all 8 message types (11 tests):
   - Frame roundtrip (header+payload) for DEVICE, PROFILE, SAFETY,
     HEALTH, FEATURE_NEGOTIATION, GAME, TELEMETRY, DIAGNOSTIC
   - Multiple frames in stream buffer parsing
   - Error response frames with combined flags
   - All flags combined in single header

4. Error propagation across IPC boundary (14 tests):
   - IO error conversions (broken pipe, refused, timed out, would block)
   - Error message preservation for all error variants
   - Send+Sync trait verification
   - Debug format non-emptiness for all 13 variants
   - Codec edge cases (empty message, garbage bytes)
   - Server double-start returns InvalidConfig

5. Concurrent client handling (6 tests):
   - 50 concurrent negotiations
   - Interleaved register/unregister (20 clients)
   - Health broadcast with 10 subscribers
   - Concurrent negotiate and stop
   - Unregister nonexistent/double-unregister

6. Large message handling (7 tests):
   - Encode at/under/over size limits
   - Exact boundary and one-byte-over
   - 64KB payload roundtrip via frame
   - encode_to_buffer clears previous content
   - encoded_len matches actual encoding

7. Proptest for message framing (4 property tests, 128 cases each):
   - Framed stream roundtrip with varying payloads
   - Header with arbitrary payload_len roundtrips
   - Version compatibility reflexive property
   - Higher minor always compatible, different major never compatible

8. Health event edge cases (5 tests):
   - Broadcast with no subscribers
   - Subscriber dropped doesn't affect others
   - Large metadata (100 entries)
   - Contiguous repr values
   - Rapid burst of 100 events

9. Server/client info edge cases (7 tests):
   - Connected clients info correctness
   - Same-ID registration overwrites
   - Features stored from negotiation
   - Default config values
   - Fresh server state

All tests return Result (no unwrap/expect), following project conventions.

- WheelCapabilities per model with torque ordering cross-check

commit.message:
fix: LogViewer reopen (LockOSThread) + DB corruption auto-repair

LogViewer: Added runtime.LockOSThread() to the LogViewer goroutine,
matching the pattern used by the main window (ui_main.go:169).
Without it, WebView2's COM message loop breaks when Go reschedules
the goroutine to a different OS thread — causing Run() to never
return on window close, permanently blocking reopening.

DB corruption: Added repairDBIfNeeded() to initHistoryDB(). Runs
PRAGMA integrity_check(1) on startup and rebuilds the FTS5 index
if corruption is detected. This fixes 'database disk image is
malformed' errors that prevented delete/merge operations when
the FTS5 external-content table was out of sync.

Co-authored-by: Copilot <223556219+Copilot@users.noreply.github.com>

Fix timeout: use socket.setdefaulttimeout(8) + 10s thread join

requests timeout= alone doesn't cap DNS resolution on Windows.
socket.setdefaulttimeout(8) applies to all socket ops (DNS+TCP+read)
and is restored after the request. Thread join reduced to 10s as the
hard cutoff that fires regardless.

Co-authored-by: Copilot <223556219+Copilot@users.noreply.github.com>

fix: remove all vim.notify from auto-load async callbacks

vim.notify in async job callbacks (even inside vim.schedule) can crash
when the user has noice.nvim or nvim-notify installed, because those
plugins replace vim.notify with a function that creates floating windows
and may not be safe at the exact moment the callback fires.

Changes:
- Auto-load (non-user-initiated) is now completely silent: no INFO
  notifications are emitted during background comment loading
- Only ERROR-level messages and user-initiated (:GitCommentsLoad)
  actions produce notifications
- Removed messy nested pcall structure; replaced with clean linear
  callback chain with a single pcall around the store/sign-refresh step
- Added a status table (per repo root) to track loading state
- nvim_buf_is_valid check added before nvim_buf_is_loaded

Co-authored-by: Copilot <223556219+Copilot@users.noreply.github.com>

commit.message:
fix: agent localhost detection, nginx params, item.params field

Three bugs fixed:

1. agent_deployer.py — duplicate class definition
   The edit tool had appended new code before old code without removing the
   old code. Python used the last (old) class definition, so the localhost
   detection and subprocess execution path were never reached.
   The file now contains only the correct single implementation.

2. Item model missing params field
   Zabbix API requires the params field for calculated, SNMP and HTTP agent
   items. Without it, item.create returns:
     Invalid parameter "/1": the parameter "params" is missing.
   Added params: str = "" to Item model.
   _create_item and _update_item in deployer.py now always send params.

3. nginx.yaml calculated item missing formula
   The nginx.requests.rate item had type: calculated but no params
   (formula). Added:
     params: "rate(/nginx/web.page.get[localhost,/nginx_status,80],1m)"

4. inventory.yaml — corrected IP for zabbixtest3100
   Was 127.0.0.1 (triggers localhost/subprocess mode on depa).
   Changed to hostname zabbixtest3100 so zbx uses SSH to reach the
   actual remote machine.

Co-authored-by: Copilot <223556219+Copilot@users.noreply.github.com>

commit.message:
fix: local file paths in confluencePages + --debug for run command

Bug 1: extractFromConfluence() crashed with 'no protocol' when
confluencePages contained a local file path (e.g. ./agents/instructions/...)
because confluence.contentByUrl() always calls new URL(), which requires
an http/https scheme. Fixed by checking for http/https prefix; non-URL
entries are now read from the local filesystem via Files.readAllBytes().
Also added null/blank guards to avoid NPE when confluencePages is null.

Bug 2: dmtools --debug run <file> silently ignored --debug because the
'run' case in dmtools.sh was handled before LOG_CONFIG was set, with
log4j2-cli.xml hardcoded. Fixed by moving LOG_CONFIG determination to
before the case block; 'run' now uses $LOG_CONFIG like all other commands.

## Summary
Ensures the landing page and all user-facing pages are responsive and
appropriate for mobile and desktop.

## Changes

### `PRDtoProd/Pages/Compliance.cshtml`
- Added `overflow-x: auto` to `.metrics-bar` so the 5-metric strip
scrolls horizontally on narrow screens instead of crushing each cell to
an unusable width

when scrolled

### `PRDtoProd/Pages/Operator.cshtml`
- Added a `@media (max-width: 640px)` breakpoint to `.boundary-grid` so
the 2-column boundary detail panel collapses to a single column on
phones

commit.message:
fix: replace deprecated vim.fn.sign_* with nvim_buf_set_extmark

The old vim.fn.sign_define/sign_place/sign_unplace API is deprecated
since Neovim 0.10 and can trigger TUI rendering crashes in Neovim 0.11
(e.g. when entering command mode). Replaced with the modern extmark API
(nvim_buf_set_extmark / nvim_buf_clear_namespace) which is stable,
crash-free, and available since Neovim 0.6.

Additional changes:
- Namespace is lazily created on first use (not at module require time)
  to avoid running Neovim API calls during plugin load
- All sign operations are wrapped with pcall for safety
- Default sign_text changed from emoji '💬' to ASCII '>>' to avoid any
  multi-byte rendering issues in the sign column

Co-authored-by: Copilot <223556219+Copilot@users.noreply.github.com>
